restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
Finally, real Neapolitan pizza that doesn't require driving into the city. This place takes pizza seriously and it shows in every bite. The crust is exactly what you want from authentic Italian pizza - thin, chewy, and with those perfect charred bubbles from their wood-fired oven. Good for date night or when you want to feel fancy about eating pizza.

The ingredients are either local or imported from Italy, which sounds pretentious but actually makes a huge difference. You can taste it in the simplest margherita pizza, but they also do more creative toppings if you're feeling adventurous. Everything comes out fast and hot.

It's in the Blue Stone shopping area, so parking is easy and you can grab gelato next door if you're still hungry. Just know that real Neapolitan pizza is supposed to be a little soggy in the middle - that's not a mistake.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This place is a lifesaver when you're craving real Indian food but don't want to deal with downtown traffic. It's in the Perimeter Center area and feels like eating at your Indian neighbor's house if they happened to be really good cooks. Perfect for family dinners or when you want to introduce friends to authentic Indian food without the intimidation factor.

The biryani is what they're known for and it's absolutely worth it. They serve everything family-style, so come hungry and ready to share. The portions are generous and the spices are on point without being overwhelming for people who aren't used to Indian food.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner most days, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. But they stay open later on weekends if you want to make it a proper dinner out.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This is my go-to when I want Middle Eastern food that doesn't cost a fortune. It's in a strip mall but the food is fresh and authentic, and they're super generous with portions. Great for a casual lunch or when you want something healthy and filling.

The shawarma is what most people order and it's really good. The falafel is crispy on the outside and fluffy inside, and they make their own pita bread daily. If you're vegetarian, this place has tons of options that actually taste good.

The staff is really friendly and patient if you're not familiar with Middle Eastern food. Prices are reasonable and you'll definitely have leftovers.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is the place you go when you need food at 2am or want a solid breakfast any time of day. It's been a Marietta institution forever, and the neon sign alone makes it worth the trip. Perfect for post-game meals, late night cravings, or weekend breakfast with the family when you don't want to wait in line somewhere trendy.

The menu is massive and they do everything pretty well, but stick to the classics. Their Greek specialties are legit since the owners are Greek, and the breakfast portions are huge. My kids love coming here because they can get pancakes while I get a gyro at 11am.

Just know it gets busy on weekend mornings, but the turnover is fast. And yes, they really are open 24 hours every day, which is rare these days.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is the real deal French bakery that makes everything from scratch daily. The owners are actually French, and you can taste the difference. Perfect for a special weekend brunch or when you want to grab fresh bread and pastries to take home.

The quiche is outstanding and changes based on what's in season. Their baguettes are as good as you'll find outside of France, and the petits fours are perfect if you need something for a special occasion. The croissants sell out early on weekends, so get there before noon.

They close early on Sundays and the space is small, so expect a wait on weekend mornings. But it moves fast and the staff is really friendly about explaining everything.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is a good place to try it without the crazy prices of some places in Atlanta. The servers come around with different meats on skewers and you just say yes or no. Perfect for a birthday dinner or when you want to try something different with friends.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip, and the feijoada stew is really authentic if you want to try traditional Brazilian food. They also serve breakfast, which is unusual for Brazilian places. The meat is good quality and they keep bringing it until you wave the white flag.

Come hungry because it's all-you-can-eat and the price reflects that. The atmosphere is relaxed and the staff explains everything if it's your first time.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This food hall is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat. Everyone can get something different from twenty different vendors all under one roof. Great for families with picky eaters or when you're meeting friends who all want different cuisines.

The variety is really impressive and the quality is solid across the board. You can get everything from tacos to sushi to BBQ, plus there are retail shops if you want to grab something to take home. It's right on the historic Marietta Square, so you can walk around after eating.

Parking can be tricky during busy times, but there are several lots nearby. It gets crowded on weekends but that's part of the fun atmosphere.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is where you go when you want to impress someone or celebrate something special. The location right on the Chattahoochee is gorgeous, especially if you can snag a table by the windows. It's definitely a splurge, but the kind of place that makes anniversaries and client dinners feel important.

The seafood is what they do best, and it shows. Everything feels refined but not stuffy, and the Southern touches keep it from feeling like you're eating in a hotel restaurant. The service is the kind where they actually know what they're talking about when you ask questions about the menu.

Reservations are pretty much required, especially for weekend dinners. They do Sunday brunch too, which is a nice middle ground if you want the experience without the full dinner price tag.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This place will make you wonder why you've been sleeping on Persian food. The atmosphere feels upscale but welcoming, perfect for girls' night out or impressing someone who thinks they've tried everything. It's the kind of restaurant that makes you feel like you discovered something special, even though it's been here for years.

The hummus and wings sound basic but they're anything but ordinary. The chicken shish kabobs with the rice that has lentils and raisins is what converts people who think they don't like Persian food. The cocktails are creative and strong, and the tiramisu is the perfect way to end the meal.

Note that they break between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. But the service is attentive and they're good about explaining dishes if you're new to Persian cuisine.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is the taco place that actually lives up to all the Instagram posts. The vibe is fun and energetic, perfect for a casual dinner with friends or when you want good Mexican food that's not just Tex-Mex. They're open late most nights, which is clutch when you get off work and still want something fresh instead of fast food.

Start with the Esquite - it's Mexican street corn that's way better than it sounds. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is dangerously good if you're drinking, and the passion fruit one is just as tasty. Their tacos are legit, but honestly everything on the menu hits.

Service is quick and the staff actually knows what they're serving, which makes trying new things less risky. It's in City Springs, so you can walk around after dinner if the weather's nice.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
